_note: Worked example: Cedar Summit Health Partners, a fictional 12-clinic ambulatory healthcare network. Practice management (ClearSlate PM) owns patients/coverage/appointments, the EHR (MeadowPoint) owns encounters/diagnoses/procedures (derived from completed appointments - real lineage), and the RCM platform (BrightBill) owns charges/claims/remittances. Two state machines run on scheduling.appointment: the scheduling lifecycle (scheduled -> checked_in -> completed / no_show / cancelled) and the visit billing lifecycle anchored on completed_at (claims with denial/resubmission). No real PII: surrogate MRNs, persona-pool names, birth_year only, 555-01xx phones, example.com emails, NPIs in the provably-fictional 9xxxxxxxxx range.

calendar._note: Ambulatory weekday shape: Monday peak, Friday taper, urgent-care-only weekends. Month weights carry the winter respiratory bump (Dec-Feb) and the mid-summer elective dip with a Nov-Dec deductible-met lift.

vocab._note: Per-category service and diagnosis pools so descriptions, fees, and chronic flags stay mutually coherent (category is drawn first, everything else conditions on it).
preventive_dx: Screening for malignant neoplasm of colon

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables._note: 12 clinics; the flagship multispecialty hub carries an outsized share of the panel via zipf clinic assignment on patients.
tables.name: clinic
tables.purpose: Clinic site master for the ambulatory network.

tables._note: Payer master with per-payer contracted rates. 'Self-Pay / Patient Direct' is a configured financial class like real PM systems use, so self-pay visits flow through the same claim machinery with zero payer payment.
tables.name: payer
tables.purpose: Payer / financial-class master with contracted reimbursement rates.

tables._note: Chargemaster: category drawn first, then description and fee both condition on it. Fees are whole-dollar (price_endings [0.0]) like a real fee schedule, which also gives downstream amounts honest duplicate mass.
tables.name: fee_schedule
tables.purpose: Chargemaster / fee schedule for billable services.

tables._note: Diagnosis reference: ICD-10-style code prefix conditions on the clinical category (J respiratory, E metabolic, I cardio, M msk, F mental health, Z preventive, S injury), as does the chronic flag.
tables.name: diagnosis_code
tables.purpose: Diagnosis code reference used by encounter coding.

tables._note: Providers carry the npi identifier kind (9xxxxxxxxx range can never be a real NPI but keeps the 80840 Luhn check digit). Clinic assignment is zipf so the hub clinic staffs the most providers, matching its patient share.
tables.name: provider
tables.purpose: Rendering providers (physicians and APPs) across the network.

tables._note: Patient master. NO real PII: surrogate MRN, persona-pool names, birth_year only (no DOB), reserved-range phone, example.com email. created_at is sorted with 70% backfill so MRN sequence correlates with registration date and the panel is tenured rather than ramping from zero. utilization_tier + a heavy-tailed per-patient multiplier on appointments produce the chronic/complex whale cohort.
tables.name: patient
tables.purpose: Practice management patient master (registration system of record).

columns.name: home_clinic_id
columns._note: zipf: the flagship hub holds roughly a third of the panel, satellites tail off.

columns.name: created_at
columns._note: sorted + backfill: registrations predate the data window for 85% of the panel, so in-window encounter volume grows at a believable ~20-30%/yr (panel growth + 5% calendar trend) instead of doubling.

tables._note: Coverage: rank 1 is the billed primary (payer mix ~ Medicare .37 / Medicaid .16 / commercial .37 / self-pay .06), rank 2+ are supplements/secondaries. payer_id uses fk affinity matching on payer_type with a 2% leak (mis-mapped plans at registration). term_date mostly null/future; the stale_mapping imperfection retro-terms 4% to feed eligibility-denial DQ-005.
tables.name: coverage
tables.purpose: Insurance coverage on file per patient.

tables._note: THE central table: every appointment, kept or not. Two machines run here. Machine 1 (scheduling) anchors on appointment_date: ~12% no-show, ~12% cancel, 1.5% never reconciled (stale 'scheduled' backlog -> DQ-010). Machine 2 (visit billing / claim lifecycle) anchors on completed_at, which machine 1 writes first - rows that never completed are skipped and legitimately stay 'not_billed'. Volume is segment-conditioned (complex patients ~12x low utilizers) with a heavy-tailed per-patient multiplier.
tables.name: appointment
tables.purpose: Appointments across all clinics, including no-shows and cancellations.

columns.name: clinic_id
columns._note: Affinity match on the patient's home clinic with a 6% leak (urgent-care walk-ins, coverage at sister sites).

columns.name: provider_id
columns._note: Provider drawn from the visit clinic's own staff with a 5% float-pool leak.

columns.name: appointment_date
columns._note: Calendar-weighted (Monday peak, near-zero weekends, winter bump) and never before the patient registered.

tables._note: Services booked WITH the appointment (schedulers book specific services), so rows legitimately exist for visits that later no-show or cancel. The fee snapshot is fk_copy'd from the chargemaster and booked_amount = fee x integer units - the required price-realism pattern. Documented procedures derive from these for completed visits only.
tables.name: appointment_service
tables.purpose: Service lines booked on each appointment, priced from the fee schedule.
tables.grain: one row per appointment per booked service line

columns.name: fee_item_id
columns._note: zipf(0.5): popular services (level-3/4 E/M, flu shots, lipid panels) lead without one code dominating.

tables.name: appointment_event
tables.purpose: Scheduling status history emitted by the appointment lifecycle machine.
tables.grain: one row per appointment per scheduling state entered

tables.name: visit_billing_event
tables.purpose: Visit billing / claim lifecycle history emitted by the claim machine.
tables.grain: one row per completed visit per billing state entered

tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Interface engine batch run log.

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (live results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.1: completed encounter with no posted charges
cases.5: claim service date after coverage termination
cases.6: staging last name disagrees with PM source
cases.8: charge posted more than 5 days after service
cases.10: past appointment never reconciled from scheduled

tables.name: control_catalog
tables.purpose: Reconciliation control catalog (break rows come from the control_recon_charge_claim view).

cases.1: Claim billed ties to posted charges
cases.3: EMPI patients tie to source mappings

tables._note: Everything below derives via SQL from the tables above - real lineage, not parallel-faked data.
tables.name: pm_patient_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly PM patient extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: pm_patient
tables.purpose: Typed/cleaned PM patients; normalizes raw extract dates.

tables.name: patient_identifier
tables.purpose: EMPI identifier crosswalk: PM MRN and EHR person id per canonical patient.

tables.name: patient
tables.purpose: Canonical (EMPI golden) patient joining staging and the crosswalk.

tables._note: Encounters exist only for appointments that actually checked in - coherence by construction, never a random FK.
tables.name: encounter
tables.purpose: EHR encounters derived 1:1 from checked-in appointments.

tables.name: diagnosis
tables.purpose: Encounter diagnoses: every encounter has a principal dx, 60% a second, 25% a third; code popularity is banded so chronic heavy-hitters dominate.
tables.grain: one row per encounter per diagnosis rank

tables.name: procedure
tables.purpose: Documented procedures: the booked services of visits that completed.
tables.grain: one row per documented service on a completed encounter

tables.name: charge
tables.purpose: Charge lines posted from documented procedures; 'pending' rows are captured but not yet posted (DNFB).
tables.grain: one row per charge line (restatement pairs append)

tables.name: claim
tables.purpose: Professional claims generated from submitted visits; status mirrors the visit billing machine, payer comes from the patient's primary coverage.
tables.grain: one row per claim (one claim per billed encounter)

tables.name: claim_line
tables.purpose: Claim lines snapshotted from posted charges at claim generation (later charge corrections create recon breaks).
tables.grain: one row per claim per line

tables.name: resubmission
tables.purpose: Corrected resubmissions of denied claims with outcome.

tables._note: Remittances derive from PAID claims. The disposition identity holds exactly by construction: paid + contractual adjustment + patient responsibility = billed. Self-pay 'remits' carry zero payer payment with the balance as patient responsibility after the uninsured discount.
tables.name: remittance
tables.purpose: 835-style remittances for paid claims with full payment disposition.
tables.grain: one row per remittance (one per paid claim)

tables.name: denial_worklist
tables.purpose: Open denial follow-up queue routed by root-cause reason; carries the registration override flag for root-cause analysis.
tables.grain: one row per open denied claim

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_patient
tables.purpose: Patient dimension from the canonical EMPI layer.

tables.name: dim_provider
tables.purpose: Provider dimension.

tables.name: fact_encounter
tables.purpose: Encounter fact with payer attribution and gross charges.

tables.name: fact_charge
tables.purpose: Charge-line fact (warehouse refresh snapshot; intentionally lags post-load charge corrections, which the recon view surfaces).
tables.grain: one row per charge line as of the warehouse refresh

tables.name: fact_claim
tables.purpose: Claim fact with adjudication outcome and payment disposition.
tables.grain: one row per claim (latest adjudication snapshot)

state_machines._note: Machine order matters: the scheduling machine writes completed_at, which the claim machine (next) anchors on. Residual 1.5% of past appointments never leave 'scheduled' - the front-desk reconciliation backlog that DQ-010 works.
state_machines.name: appointment_lifecycle

state_machines._note: Claim lifecycle on the same table, anchored on completed_at: rows that never completed are skipped and stay 'not_billed'. Denial rate ~11% of adjudicated; denied claims resubmit (45%), appeal (20%), or write off (28%), with residual open denials feeding the worklist. Right-censoring leaves recent claims mid-adjudication (open AR).
state_machines.name: claim_lifecycle

derivations.name: raw_pm_patient_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; the post-derivation format_drift imperfection corrupts a slice, which DQ-004 catches.
sql: select cast(patient_id as text), mrn, first_name, last_name, sex, cast(birth_year as text),

derivations.name: stg_pm_patient
sql: select cast(patient_id as integer), mrn, trim(first_name), trim(last_name), upper(sex),

derivations.name: xref_patient_identifier
derivations._note: A crosswalk that maps a single system is not a crosswalk - both PM and EHR identifiers are mapped; missing_xref later deletes ~2% to feed DQ-003.
sql: select row_number() over (order by patient_id), 'patient', patient_id, 'ClearSlate PM',
select 400000 + row_number() over (order by patient_id), 'patient', patient_id, 'MeadowPoint EHR',

derivations.name: core_patient
sql: select s.patient_id, s.mrn, s.first_name || ' ' || s.last_name, s.sex, s.birth_year,
where x.canonical_patient_id = s.patient_id and x.source_system = 'MeadowPoint EHR'
from stg.pm_patient s join patient.patient p on p.patient_id = s.patient_id;

derivations.name: clinical_encounter
sql: select a.appointment_id, a.appointment_id, a.patient_id, a.provider_id, a.clinic_id,
case when a.completed_at is not null then 'completed' else 'open' end,
case when a.completed_at is not null

derivations.name: clinical_diagnosis
derivations._note: Deterministic id-modulo variety (no random() in derivations). Code popularity is banded: the top 15 codes take 45% of principal diagnoses, ranks 2/3 draw from the mid and tail bands so codes rarely repeat within an encounter.

derivations.name: clinical_procedure
derivations._note: Documented procedures = the booked service lines of visits that completed. fk_copy fee x units flows through from scheduling.appointment_service.
sql: select s.service_id, s.appointment_id, s.fee_item_id, s.charge_code, s.service_category,
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = s.appointment_id

derivations.name: billing_charge
derivations._note: posted_at follows the billing machine's charges_entered_at with per-line jitter; visits stuck before charge entry stay 'pending' (the DNFB story DQ-001 reports).
sql: select p.procedure_id, p.encounter_id, e.patient_id, cov.payer_id, p.service_date,
case when a.charges_entered_at is not null
case when a.charges_entered_at is not null then 'posted' else 'pending' end,
join clinical.encounter e on e.encounter_id = p.encounter_id
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = p.encounter_id
left join patient.coverage cov on cov.patient_id = e.patient_id and cov.coverage_rank = 1;

derivations.name: claims_claim
derivations._note: Claim status maps from the machine's billing_status; denial_reason exists iff the claim was ever denied, with eligibility denials over-represented when registration overrode a failed eligibility check (root-cause coherence).
sql: select a.appointment_id, 'CLM-' || printf('%07d', a.appointment_id), a.appointment_id, a.patient_id,
case when a.denied_at is null then null
join patient.coverage cov on cov.patient_id = a.patient_id and cov.coverage_rank = 1

derivations.name: claims_claim_line
sql: row_number() over (partition by cl.claim_id order by c.charge_id),
join claims.claim cl on cl.encounter_id = c.encounter_id

derivations.name: claims_resubmission
sql: case when a.paid_at is not null then 'paid'

derivations.name: claims_remittance
derivations._note: Disposition identity is exact by construction: contractual = billed - allowed; patient responsibility by payer type (20% coinsurance Medicare, copay-capped commercial, full balance self-pay); paid = allowed - patient responsibility.
sql: select cl.claim_id, cl.payer_id, cl.billed_amount, date(a.paid_at) as remit_date,
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = cl.claim_id
join ref.payer py on py.payer_id = cl.payer_id
join patient.coverage cov on cov.coverage_id = cl.coverage_id
select claim_id, '835-' || printf('%07d', claim_id), claim_id, payer_id, remit_date,
case claim_id % 5 when 0 then 'check' else 'EFT' end

derivations.name: workflow_denial_worklist
derivations._note: Open denials only (stuck 'denied' plus exhausted 'denied_final'); queue routes by root cause, assignees are a small fictional biller pool.
sql: select cl.claim_id, cl.claim_id, cl.denial_reason, cl.payer_id, cl.billed_amount,
case cl.claim_id % 6 when 0 then 'D. Acharya' when 1 then 'M. Ferreira'
when 2 then 'K. Boswell' when 3 then 'T. Nakagawa'
when 4 then 'R. Ellison' else 'S. Mwangi' end,
case when cl.claim_status = 'denied_final' and cl.claim_id % 3 = 0 then 'escalated' else 'open' end
join scheduling.appointment a on a.appointment_id = cl.claim_id

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_patient
sql: left join ref.clinic cl on cl.clinic_id = c.home_clinic_id;

derivations.name: wh_dim_provider
sql: select p.provider_id, p.provider_name, p.npi, p.credential, p.specialty, c.clinic_name
left join ref.clinic c on c.clinic_id = p.clinic_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_encounter
sql: select e.encounter_id, e.encounter_date, e.patient_id, e.provider_id, e.clinic_id,
(select count(*) from clinical.procedure p where p.encounter_id = e.encounter_id),
left join patient.coverage cov on cov.patient_id = e.patient_id and cov.coverage_rank = 1;

derivations.name: wh_fact_charge
sql: select c.charge_id, c.service_date, date(c.posted_at), c.patient_id, e.provider_id,
join clinical.encounter e on e.encounter_id = c.encounter_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_claim
sql: select cl.claim_id, cl.submitted_date, cl.service_date, cl.patient_id, cl.payer_id,
case when r.remit_date is not null
left join claims.remittance r on r.claim_id = cl.claim_id;

derivations.name: mart_clinic_volume_daily
derivations._note: Mart as a VIEW so it reflects post-imperfection data. Shows the weekday-clinic shape and the ~12% no-show rate by site.
sql: sum(case when a.status = 'completed' then 1 else 0 end) as visits_completed,
sum(case when a.status = 'no_show' then 1 else 0 end) as no_shows,
sum(case when a.status = 'cancelled' then 1 else 0 end) as cancellations,
round(1.0 * sum(case when a.status = 'no_show' then 1 else 0 end) /
nullif(count(*) - sum(case when a.status = 'cancelled' then 1 else 0 end), 0), 3) as no_show_rate
join ref.clinic c on c.clinic_id = a.clinic_id

derivations.name: mart_revenue_cycle_monthly
sql: sum(case when cl.denial_reason is not null then 1 else 0 end) as claims_ever_denied,
round(1.0 * sum(case when cl.denial_reason is not null then 1 else 0 end) /
nullif(sum(case when cl.claim_status in ('paid','denied','denied_final','written_off','resubmitted','appealed') then 1 else 0 end), 0), 3) as denial_rate,
round(avg(case when r.remit_date is not null
then julianday(r.remit_date) - julianday(cl.submitted_date) end), 1) as avg_days_to_pay
left join claims.remittance r on r.claim_id = cl.claim_id

derivations.name: control_recon_charge_claim
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW: claim lines are a snapshot at claim generation, while billing.charge keeps moving (post-load restatement pairs and interface drops) - exactly how real charge-to-claim recons break.
sql: select cl.claim_id, cl.encounter_id, cl.billed_amount as claim_billed,
round(coalesce(sum(case when c.charge_status = 'posted' then c.charge_amount end), 0), 2) as charges_posted_now,
round(cl.billed_amount - coalesce(sum(case when c.charge_status = 'posted' then c.charge_amount end), 0), 2) as break_amount
left join billing.charge c on c.encounter_id = cl.encounter_id
having abs(cl.billed_amount - round(coalesce(sum(case when c.charge_status = 'posted' then c.charge_amount end), 0), 2)) > 0.01;

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'completed encounter with no posted charges' as issue, cast(e.encounter_id as text) as entity_id
where e.visit_status = 'completed' and e.encounter_date <= date('2025-12-31', '-10 days')
and not exists (select 1 from billing.charge c where c.encounter_id = e.encounter_id and c.charge_status = 'posted')
select 'DQ-002', 'procedure references missing encounter', cast(p.procedure_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from clinical.encounter e where e.encounter_id = p.encounter_id)
select 'DQ-003', 'patient missing source identifier mapping', cast(p.patient_id as text)
where (select count(distinct x.source_system) from xref.patient_identifier x
from raw.pm_patient_extract r where r.registration_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-005', 'claim service date after coverage termination', cast(cl.claim_id as text)
from claims.claim cl join patient.coverage cov on cov.coverage_id = cl.coverage_id
where cov.term_date is not null and cl.service_date > cov.term_date
select 'DQ-006', 'staging last name disagrees with PM source', cast(s.patient_id as text)
from stg.pm_patient s join patient.patient p on p.patient_id = s.patient_id
select 'DQ-007', 'duplicate interface job delivery', cast(jr.run_id as text)
where j2.job_name = jr.job_name and j2.run_started_at = jr.run_started_at and j2.run_id <> jr.run_id)
select 'DQ-008', 'charge posted more than 5 days after service', cast(c.charge_id as text)
where c.posted_at is not null and julianday(c.posted_at) - julianday(c.service_date) > 5
select 'DQ-009', 'possible duplicate patient registration', cast(p.patient_id as text)
where q.first_name = p.first_name and q.birth_year = p.birth_year
and q.city = p.city and q.patient_id <> p.patient_id)
select 'DQ-010', 'past appointment never reconciled from scheduled', cast(a.appointment_id as text)
where a.status = 'scheduled' and a.appointment_date <= date('2025-12-31', '-7 days');

imperfections.name: patient_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Hand-keyed registrations accumulate name typos; they propagate through the extract into staging and the EMPI.

imperfections.name: duplicate_patient_registrations
imperfections.scenario: Front desk re-registers patients instead of searching; DQ-009 surfaces the merge candidates for the EMPI queue.

imperfections.name: coverage_stale_terms
imperfections.scenario: Coverage termed by the employer but still on file at registration; claims billed against it trip DQ-005 (eligibility denials story).

imperfections.name: registration_eligibility_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Registration bypasses failed eligibility checks; the denial worklist carries the flag and those visits skew toward eligibility denials.

imperfections.name: duplicate_job_runs
imperfections.scenario: Scheduler retries log duplicate interface runs; DQ-007 dedups them.

imperfections.name: raw_extract_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy extract job pads dates after a patch; DQ-004 catches the unparseable slice.

imperfections.name: stg_name_conflicts
imperfections.scenario: EHR-sourced demographic merge mangles some staged names vs the PM source; DQ-006 reports the disagreement.

imperfections.name: empi_xref_gaps
imperfections.scenario: Mappings lost during the 2024 EMPI migration; DQ-003 counts patients with incomplete crosswalks.

imperfections.name: orphan_procedures
imperfections.scenario: An EHR encounter purge corrupted procedure links; DQ-002 is the orphan-procedure queue.

imperfections.name: charge_feed_drops
imperfections.scenario: Charge interface drops lines after claims were generated - encounters lose charges (DQ-001) and claim-vs-charge recon breaks (CTL-001).

imperfections.name: charge_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Charge corrections issued as reversal + restated pairs after claim generation; these create the CTL-001 recon breaks.

imperfections.name: late_charge_posts
imperfections.scenario: Late charge entry beyond the 5-day standard; DQ-008 tracks the late-charge rate.

controls.rule_name: Claim billed ties to posted charges

controls.expected_break_rate: 0 (identity by construction: paid + contractual + patient = billed)

controls.rule_name: EMPI patients tie to source mappings

dq_rules.condition: completed encounters have posted charges within 10 days
every patient mapped in both PM and EHR
claim service date within coverage window
staged last name matches PM source
one delivery per job per start time
charges post within 5 days of service
no duplicate first name + birth year + city registrations

dataflows.name: patient_mastering
dataflows.sequence: patient.patient -> raw.pm_patient_extract -> stg.pm_patient -> xref.patient_identifier -> core.patient -> wh.dim_patient

dataflows.name: visit_to_cash
dataflows.sequence: scheduling.appointment (+services) -> scheduling machine -> clinical.encounter/diagnosis/procedure -> billing.charge -> claims.claim/claim_line -> claim machine -> claims.remittance -> wh facts -> marts

dataflows.name: denial_management
dataflows.sequence: claim machine (denied) -> claims.claim.denial_reason -> workflow.denial_worklist -> claims.resubmission / appeal outcomes
